rulings.Case Title: NATIONWIDE LEGAL INC. VS. WILLIAM D. DOUGLASS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent William Douglass' Motion For Sanctions (No Stipulation To Jpt At 8/13/12 Hearing)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 27, 2012, Line 12, Respondent William Douglass' Motion For Sanctions (No Stipulation To Jpt At 8/13/12 Hearing). The court adopts, in part, the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Halperin as follows: Continued to August 31, 2012 at 9:30 am in Department 302. Respondent shall file and serve a supplemental brief and supporting evidence that shall be organized in a way for the court to segregate by category the amounts from the total claimed, including itemizations for: (1) opposing Nationwide's motion for summary judgment; and (2) creating Respondent's analysis of mileage driven by the deceased before Nationwide produced the dispatcher's logs. Such supplement brief shall be filed no later than August 27, 2012 at 4 pm. Petitioner may file and serve a responsive brief no later than August 28, 2012 at 4 pm. Both briefs shall be limited to no more than 5 pages. Service shall be effectuated by email, fax or personal delivery. Courtesy copies shall be provided to Department 302 on the date of filing. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND D. JACKSON SR. VS. DR. STEVEN PLETCHER et al (Provide Access)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 27, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANTs STEVEN PLETCHER, M.D., THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is granted. Defendants has met its summary judgment burden based on the declaration of Dr. Kaplan. Defendant met the standard of care. Plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of material fact. The opinion of Dr. Lopchinsky is not under penalty of perjury and does not comply with CCP 2015.5. The Court sustains Defendant's objection to Dr. Lopchinsky's letter opinion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SOUTHPEAK INTERACTIVE, LLC, A VIRGINIA LIMITED VS. DEEP SILVER, INC.,A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 27, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT DEEP SILVER, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (PART 1 OF 3 OF TENTATIVE RULING). The motion is off calendar as moot as to the third and seventh causes of action, which were dismissed by Plaintiff on July 26, 2012. As to all remaining causes of action, the motion for summary judgment is granted and the alternative motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as moot. The first cause of action for rescission fails because Defendant shifted the burden to show that Plaintiff cannot prove duress, fraud or mistake and Plaintiff failed to raise a triable issue. Regarding duress, Defendant presented uncontested evidence that at the time of this meeting it still had the right under the original Agreement to terminate. Threatening to do what Defendant already had a right to do is not wrongful or grounds for duress. (See Burke v Gould (1894) 105 Cal. 277.) Regarding fraud, the Agreement, and First Amendment thereto, are fully integrated and both of the misrepresentations alleged by Plaintiff are covered by and contradict the terms of the Agreement and First Amendment. (See Def.'s Ex. 1 and 2, respectively.) Thus, the misrepresentations are inadmissible under the parol evidence rule. (See Bank of America v. Pendergrass (1935) 4 Cal.2d 258, 263; Alling v. Universal Mfg. Corp. (1992) 5 Cal.App.4th 1412, 1436.) Regarding mistake, Plaintiff cannot establish mistake based on its own misunderstanding of the meaning of the contract. (See Hedging Concepts, Inc. v. First Alliance Mortgage Co. (1996) 41 Cal.App.4th 1410, 1421.) To the extent that the "mistake" was induced by the alleged misrepresentation of Defendant, such evidence is inadmissible as discussed above. *TENTATIVE RULING CONTINUED* =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SOUTHPEAK INTERACTIVE, LLC, A VIRGINIA LIMITED VS. DEEP SILVER, INC.,A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Deep Silver, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Part 2 Of 3 Of Tentative Ruling).
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 27, 2012, Line 5, Defendant Deep Silver, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Part 2 Of 3 Of Tentative Ruling). The second cause of action for fraud fails because neither of Plaintiff's alleged misrepresentations is actionable for the reasons discussed above. The fourth cause of action for breach of contract fails because it is pled assuming Plaintiff's first cause of action for rescission is viable. As discussed above, the first cause of action is not viable. The fifth cause of action for breach of contract fails because Defendant shifted the burden by showing that the damages sought by Plaintiff are excluded from recovery by §§ 7.7 and 15.9 of the Agreement and Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue. The sixth cause of action for unjust enrichment fails for two reasons. First, unjust enrichment is merely restitution by another name and a court will not imply a contract and award restitution that is contrary to the terms of the parties' express contract. (Wal-Noon Corporation v. Hill (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 605, 613; Hedging Concepts, Inc. v. First Alliance Mortgage Co. (1996) 41 Cal.App.4th 1410, 1419-1420.) Defendant has shifted the burden to show that the Agreement spelled out the terms of Plaintiff's compensation and limited the damages Plaintiff is seeking under this cause of action. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue. Second, one cannot recover restitution for doing what they were contractually obligation to do. (See Major-Blankeney v. Jenkins (1953) 121 Cal.App.2d 325; Durell v. Sharp Healthcare (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 1350.) Defendant shifted the burden by showing that Plaintiff did nothing to market, promote, or sell Dead Island beyond using its best efforts, which Plaintiff was already contractually obligated to do. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue. *TENTATIVE RULING CONTINUED* =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Joseph Camillocci'S Motion For Temporary Protective Order, Permanent Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 27, 2012, Line 15, Plaintiff Joseph Camillocci'S Motion For Temporary Protective Order, Permanent Protective Order. Plaintiff's Motion for Protective Order granted in part and denied in part. The Court understands that the parties are in agreement as to the terms of a protective order except for the following two provisions, on which the Court now rules. Defendant's proposed paragraph 2.2.9 shall be included in the protective order. Defendant's proposed paragraph 8.1 shall be included in the protective order, requiring that "At the conclusion of this litigation, all Confidential patient medical records and files shall be returned to counsel of record for the Deisgnation Party." The balance of paragraph 8.1 of the agreed upon Protective Order shall remain as written. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
